description: Production sprint: forgot password, research session 20/20 fix, WebSocket stability, remove hardcoded values, food tab context, food emotion graph, iOS deployment.

userStories.title: Add password_reset_tokens table to Drizzle schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a password_reset_tokens table so users can securely reset their password via a time-limited email token.
acceptanceCriteria: Add passwordResetTokens table to shared/schema.ts with columns: id (serial pk), userId (integer, references users), token (text, unique), expiresAt (timestamp), usedAt (timestamp nullable), createdAt (timestamp default now)
Run drizzle-kit push or generate migration file successfully
Table is exported from shared/schema.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Resend email dependency and forgot-password API endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive a password reset email so I can recover my account when I forget my password.
acceptanceCriteria: resend package added to package.json dependencies
RESEND_API_KEY added to .env.example (not hardcoded)
POST /api/auth/forgot-password endpoint added to server/routes.ts
Endpoint accepts { email } body, looks up user by email, generates crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex') token
Token stored in passwordResetTokens table with expiresAt = now + 1 hour
Email sent via Resend with reset link: https://dream-analysis.vercel.app/reset-password?token=TOKEN
Endpoint always returns 200 with { message: 'If that email exists, a reset link was sent' } (no user enumeration)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Requires RESEND_API_KEY env var. Set in Vercel dashboard and local .env.

userStories.title: Add reset-password API endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to set a new password using the token from my email so I can regain access to my account.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/reset-password endpoint added to server/routes.ts
Endpoint accepts { token, newPassword } body
Looks up token in passwordResetTokens where token matches AND expiresAt > now AND usedAt IS NULL
If not found or expired: returns 400 with { message: 'Invalid or expired reset token' }
If valid: hashes newPassword with bcrypt, updates users table, marks token usedAt = now
Returns 200 with { message: 'Password updated successfully' }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add forgot-password and reset-password pages to frontend
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'Forgot password?' link on the login page and a flow to enter my email and then set a new password.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Forgot password?' link added below the login form in client/src/pages/auth.tsx
Link navigates to /forgot-password route
client/src/pages/forgot-password.tsx created: email input form, submits to POST /api/auth/forgot-password, shows success message after submit
client/src/pages/reset-password.tsx created: reads ?token= from URL, shows new password + confirm password inputs, submits to POST /api/auth/reset-password, redirects to /auth on success with toast
Both routes added to App.tsx (no auth required)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Flatten research stress session to 20 minutes with conditional breathing
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the stress session to be exactly 20 minutes with breathing only shown when the user self-reports stress, so participants aren't forced through unnecessary exercises.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/pages/StudySessionStress.tsx (or equivalent), total session duration set to 20 minutes
Remove mandatory baseline (5 min) and recovery (5 min) phases — keep task phase only, padded to 20 min
Mid-session check-in asks: 'Are you feeling stressed right now?' (yes/no)
If yes → show breathing exercise component (box breathing, 3 min) then continue
If no → skip breathing exercise entirely, continue session
Timer counts down from 20:00
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Flatten research food session to 20 minutes with conditional breathing
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the food session to be exactly 20 minutes with the same conditional breathing logic as the stress session.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/pages/StudySessionFood.tsx (or equivalent), total session duration set to 20 minutes
Remove mandatory baseline and recovery phases
Mid-session check-in: 'Are you feeling stressed right now?' — same conditional breathing logic as US-005
Timer counts down from 20:00
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implement after US-005 to reuse the same pattern.

userStories.title: Fix WebSocket reconnect — unlimited retries with keepalive ping
userStories.description: As a user with a Muse headset, I want the app to keep trying to reconnect to the WebSocket indefinitely so I don't lose my session when the connection briefly drops.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/hooks/use-device.tsx, MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS removed or set to Infinity — reconnect loop never gives up while isStreamingRef.current === true
Add 30-second WebSocket ping: send JSON {type:'ping'} every 30s when WS is open to prevent idle timeout
Clear ping interval on ws.onclose and ws.onerror
Add reconnectCount state exposed from hook (for UI display)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add 'Reconnecting...' banner to EEG streaming pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a visible reconnecting indicator when the WebSocket drops so I know the app is trying to recover and I don't need to manually refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: reconnectCount exposed from useDevice hook
When state === 'streaming' AND reconnectCount > 0: amber banner shown at top of brain-monitor.tsx, emotion-lab.tsx, and device-setup.tsx with text 'Reconnecting to EEG stream… (attempt N)'
Banner disappears when reconnectCount resets to 0 (connection restored)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove hardcoded metric baselines from dashboard — show 0 or '--' when no EEG
userStories.description: As a user, I want dashboard metrics to show 0 or '--' when no EEG data is available, not fake simulation values like 28 or 0.25, so I can trust what I'm seeing.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/pages/dashboard.tsx, remove all simulation baseline values (e.g. 0.25 stress, 0.4 focus)
When latestFrame is null: all EEG-derived metrics display '--' or 0
No metric ever shows a non-zero number unless it came from a real EEG frame
Food emotion percentages (e.g. hardcoded 17%) replaced with '--' when no live data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fix food log tab context — change description per meal type
userStories.description: As a user logging food, I want the description prompt to change when I switch between Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, and Snack tabs so the context feels relevant.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/pages/food-log.tsx (or food-emotion.tsx), identify the tab component (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner / Snack)

Each tab has its own unique description/prompt text: Breakfast='What did you have for breakfast?', Lunch='What did you eat for lunch?', Dinner='What did you have for dinner?', Snack='Any snacks today?'
Description text updates immediately when tab is changed — does not require save/submit
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Restore food emotion breakdown graph — what you wanted to eat
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a breakdown chart of food cravings/appetite states (like how much I wanted to eat and what kind) so I can track patterns over time.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/pages/food-emotion.tsx, add a bar or radar chart below the current food state display
Chart shows the 6 food state distribution from the last 7 days of food emotion logs (from DB query)
Chart uses Recharts BarChart or RadarChart (already in dependencies)
Chart title: 'Food Craving Pattern — Last 7 Days'
If no data: shows empty state 'Log food sessions to see your craving patterns'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire food emotion percentages to live EEG data from latest frame
userStories.description: As a user, I want food emotion percentages to reflect what the EEG is actually detecting in real time, not a static value like 17% that never changes.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/pages/food-emotion.tsx, import useDevice hook and read latestFrame
Food state percentages derived from latestFrame.analysis.emotions.probabilities (or equivalent field)
When latestFrame is null: show '--' for all percentages
When latestFrame is present: map happy/sad/focus/stress emotion probabilities to food states (craving_carbs = high stress, balanced = high relaxation, etc.) using the existing food state enum
No hardcoded 17% or any other fixed percentage
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Food state mapping: stress_eating = stress_index > 0.6, mindful_eating = relaxation_index > 0.6, comfort_seeking = valence < -0.2, balanced = all indices in 0.3-0.6 range.

userStories.title: Verify Capacitor iOS build configuration and run cap sync
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm the iOS build is configured correctly (bundle ID, signing, entitlements) so the app can be archived for TestFlight.
acceptanceCriteria: Run npm run build — exits 0
Run npx cap sync ios — exits 0 with no errors
capacitor.config.ts appId is com.neuraldreamworkshop.app
ios/App/App.xcodeproj exists and opens correctly
Info.plist contains NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription and NSBluetoothPeripheralUsageDescription for Muse BLE
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is code/config only. The actual Xcode archive + TestFlight upload must be done manually by Sravya in Xcode (Product > Archive > Distribute App > TestFlight).

userStories.title: Add iOS build instructions to README and update STATUS.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear iOS build steps documented so the process is repeatable without needing to figure it out each time.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md iOS section added: npm run build → npx cap sync ios → open Xcode → Product > Archive → Distribute App → App Store Connect → TestFlight
STATUS.md updated: iOS section marked as 'Build verified, TestFlight pending' with today's date
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sravya must manually complete the Xcode archive step. This story just ensures the build pipeline works and is documented.

userStories.title: Migrate ML backend from Render (sleeps) to Railway (always-on WebSocket)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the ML backend to stay awake 24/7 so WebSocket connections never drop due to cold starts and the Muse headset connects instantly every time.
acceptanceCriteria: ml/railway.json verified: builder is DOCKERFILE, dockerfilePath is ml/Dockerfile, healthcheckPath is /health, startCommand is 'uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT'
ml/Dockerfile exists and builds successfully locally: docker build -f ml/Dockerfile . exits 0
CORS_ORIGINS in ml/main.py default string includes https://dream-analysis.vercel.app and https://dream-analysis-*.vercel.app
Document Railway deployment steps in CLAUDE.md under 'ML Backend URL Chain': install Railway CLI → railway login → railway link → railway up
Update VITE_ML_API_URL in Vercel env vars to Railway service URL after deployment
Update start.sh comment: note Railway as the production backend, ngrok only for local dev
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Railway free tier: $5/mo credit. Sravya must: 1) Create account at railway.app, 2) Run 'railway login && railway link' in terminal, 3) Run 'railway up' from repo root. The railway.json already exists — deployment is one command. After deploy, copy the Railway service URL and update VITE_ML_API_URL in Vercel dashboard + run 'vercel --prod'.
